sources.name: Department for Education
sources.dataset: Early Years Foundation Stage Profile results

sources.name: Education Policy Institute
sources.dataset: Annual Report on Education in England

metadata.methodology: Good Level of Development (GLD) is the headline measure from the Early Years Foundation Stage Profile (EYFSP), assessed by teachers at the end of Reception (age 5). A child achieves GLD if they reach at least the expected level in all 17 Early Learning Goals across communication and language, physical development, personal/social/emotional development, literacy and mathematics. No assessments took place in 2020 or 2021 due to COVID-19 school closures. The framework was revised in 2021/22, making direct comparisons with pre-2022 data approximate. The disadvantage gap uses free school meal (FSM) eligibility as the deprivation indicator.
knownIssues: No EYFSP data for 2020 or 2021 academic years due to COVID-19 cancellations
Revised EYFSP framework from 2021/22 means pre- and post-2022 figures are not directly comparable
FSM eligibility as a deprivation proxy may undercount some disadvantaged children
